DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Roderick Bushnell, esq. (415-217-3803) for Plaintiff Megan Hayenga via court call.
Benjamin R. Buchwalter, esq. (650-614-7400) of Orrick...& Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant Williams-Sonoma, Inc via court call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the second, fourth, and ninth causes of action and overruled as to the first, third, fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth, and tenth causes of action.

(see word minutes for full details)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Roderick Bushnell, esq. (415-217-3803) for Plaintiff Megan Hayenga via court call.
Benjamin R. Buchwalter, esq. (650-614-7400) of Orrick...& Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant Williams-Sonoma, Inc via court call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in its entirety.

Defendant's motion to strike punitive damage claims is denied. Punitive damages are allowed in FEHA claims, and Defendant does not show that Plaintiff's allegations fail to rise to the requisite level of fraud, malice, or oppression. "[W]rongful termination, without more, will not sustain a finding of malice or oppression." (Scott v. Phoenix Sch., Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 702, 717 [holding punitive damages are not allowed where the "only evidence of wrongful conduct directed toward [the employee] was her termination for an improper reason."].) Here, however, Plaintiff also alleges that she was discriminated against on the basis of her sex and age, which can constitute a basis for punitive damages. (See e.g. Cloud v. Casey (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 895, 912 ["The jury could properly conclude that the [defendant] intentionally discriminated by denying [plaintiff] a promotion based on gender, then attempted to hide the illegal reason for their decision with a false explanation, and that in this, they acted in a manner that was base, contemptible or vile. The discriminatory employment decision denied [plaintiff] her protected right under FEHA to seek, obtain, and hold employment without discrimination or abridgment on account of sex."].)

Defendant's motion to strike attorney fees is denied, as an award of attorneys' fees is authorized for both UCL and Lab. Code 1102.5 claims. (Yanting Zhang v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 57 Cal.4th 364, 371, fn. 4 [although the UCL does not provide for attorney fees, a prevailing plaintiff may seek attorney fees as a private attorney general under Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5]; Cordero-Sacks v. Hous. Auth. Of the City of L.A. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1267, 1285 [awarding attorney fees for claims that included Lab. Code 1102.5]; Hawkins v. City of L.A. (2015) 40 Cal.App.5th 384, 397 [awarding attorney fees for a Lab. Code 1102.5 claim under Code of Civ. Proc. 1021.5].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
